title: [lora_l2.c:555-556] LOG_INF frequency in Hz but comment says MHz
description:
Round 2 gap analysis: Missed because it's a cosmetic issue.

At line 555-556:
```c
LOG_INF("LoRa L2 started: SF10, BW125, %uMHz, %ddBm",
        CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY / 1000000, CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_TX_POWER);
```

If CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY is 915000000, dividing by 1000000 gives 915, and the log says '915MHz' which is correct. However, this integer division truncates - if the frequency were 915500000 (915.5 MHz), the log would say '915MHz' losing the fractional MHz.

Very minor - most LoRa frequencies are whole MHz values. But if fractional MHz support is ever needed, this logging would be misleading.
close_reason: Fixed: changed log to show full precision with kHz (e.g. 915.000MHz)
